source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Eugene Island Block 252

event.top_event: Personnel injury from crane lifting operation
actions_taken: Deckhand sent to physician for treatment
Investigation team reviewed Job Safety Analysis and crane procedures
event.summary: On 16-JAN-2020 at 0530 hours, a deckhand sustained two fractured ribs while attempting to retrieve a Job Safety Analysis from a personnel basket being lifted by crane on M/V Mr. Lloyd at Eugene Island 252-I. The crane operator lifted the basket while the deckhand was still exiting it, causing the deckhand to lose balance and strike the midship bitt of the vessel. The incident resulted from failure to follow established hand signal procedures, obstructed crane operator visibility, and ambiguous radio communication.
key_phrases: crane operator could not see personnel basket or deckhand
verbal communication on open marine channel
designated signal person not in clear view

hazards.name: Ambiguous or uncontrolled communication during lifting

threats.name: Crane operator initiates lift without clear signal from designated signal person
Personnel in or exiting personnel basket during unplanned lift
Crane operator receives radio command from unidentified source on open marine channel

consequences.name: Personnel struck by equipment or structure
Lost time accident requiring medical treatment

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for crane operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to establish procedures for safe lifting

supporting_text: The crane operator did not follow the Job Safety Analysis procedures while lifting the personnel basket
According to the Job Safety Analysis, the crane operator was to follow hand signals from the deckhand
There was no listing on the Job Safety Analysis for verbal communication

controls.name: Designated signal person with clear visibility
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring designated signal person in clear view of crane operator

supporting_text: The crane operator stated he could not see the deckhand or the personnel basket at the time of the lift
The crane operator stated he could not see the personnel basket or the deckhand at time he began to lift due to the design of the platform deck blocking his view of the boat
Be in clear view of the Crane Operator to ensure that their signals may be seen

controls.name: Hand signal communication protocol
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control establishing clear hand signal procedures between crane operator and signal person

supporting_text: Signals between the Crane Operator and the designated signal person should be discernible, audibly or visually, at all times
The crane operator should not respond unless signals are clearly understood
The crane operator stated he heard the command to 'come up' on the radio prior to lifting but could not determine who was speaking

controls.name: Lift plan revision when conditions change
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring revision of lift plan when original plan cannot be followed

supporting_text: The crane operator failed to follow Arena's Safe Practices Manual concerning crane operations. According to the lift planning section, the crane operator should have revised the original lift plan
A lift plan should be created before each crane operation and adhered to during the entire operation. If the lift plan cannot be followed, it must be revised

controls.name: Radio communication protocol on dedicated channel
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent ambiguous radio commands from external sources

supporting_text: At the time of the incident, the radios were set on an open marine channel allowing the crane operator to receive communication from other facilities
The crane operator stated he received the signal by radio to 'come up' with the personnel basket but could not determine who gave the command

controls.name: Secondary signal person when primary visibility obstructed
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring secondary signal person when crane operator cannot see primary signal person

supporting_text: If the Crane Operator's view of the primary signal person is obstructed, a secondary signal person should be provided

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
